target: Cross-user clustering and anonymization for veld

lens_a_edgewalk.top_finding: EntityNode.name stores person names plaintext in graph (severity 10)
structural_prerequisites: Build global vector index for cross-user queries
Cross-user retrieval pipeline (vector only, no BM25)
GDPR cascade delete for derived data

lens_b_hde.top_finding: Collective Hebbian Prior — share graph topology, not content (severity 9)
lens_b_hde.unifying_insight: System already has every primitive needed for privacy-preserving cross-user intelligence: Hebbian graph, tiered consolidation, interference detector, Voronoi analysis, MIF/PII, A/B testing. They need composition, not creation.
lens_b_hde.proposed_pipeline: Collect → Consolidate → Anonymize → Aggregate → Transfer → Measure

lens_c_clustering.top_finding: No User entity in type system — user is implicit string (6 missing mediators identified)

top_rpn.finding: MCP/Rust auth model inverts at process boundary

unifying_patterns: Privacy Inversion: every Hebbian mechanism proposed as privacy mitigation inverts to become privacy amplifier for highest-salience data
Two-Graph Prerequisite: four cross-products require the same fix — a second anonymized graph path
Process Boundary Is Security Boundary: Rust server security is bypassed by MCP deployment reality

top_rpn.finding: No User type = auth/identity crisis at every layer

ac_products.structural_meta_finding: Filesystem isolation is the ONLY effective security mechanism. Type system, auth, ML pipeline, and graph DB all operate as if single-user. Any path bypassing filesystem boundary destroys all isolation.

top_rpn.finding: LearnedWeights evicted silently — population prior impossible

bc_products.unifying_observation: Isolation boundary is in right place for security but is undifferentiated barrier rather than selective membrane. All high-RPN findings reduce to: run_maintenance_all_users() never writes upward to a shared store. System needs ONE new scope level (collective/ directory), not six separate mediators.

resonances.name: Graph Entity PII Persistence

ac: XP-6 (cluster split caused encryption gap, RPN 486)

resonances.three_body_system: Memory decay × Graph persistence × Encryption scope — three independent systems that must synchronize on PII lifecycle but currently don't interact

resonances.name: Missing Population Aggregation Layer
resonances.three_body_system: MultiUserMemoryManager × run_maintenance_all_users() × per-user MemorySystem — coordinator/aggregator/store triangle with no upward signal flow

ac: XP-3 (auth at transport not storage, RPN 216)
bc: implicit in F4 (no per-user persistence identity)

resonances.name: Embedding-Space Privacy
resonances.three_body_system: Embedding quality × Privacy budget × Knowledge transfer effectiveness — conservation triangle
resonances.atam_tension: Knowledge sharing (rich embeddings) vs. Privacy (inversion attacks)

ab: XP-12 (SemanticFact is primitive, no trigger, RPN 360)
ac: XP-8 (GDPR delete misses model weights, RPN 378)

challenges.adversary_argument: veld is single-user; user owns their own PII; graph persistence is a feature
challenges.verdict: SURVIVES — valid for cross-user target question
challenges.key_evidence: forget_user(), list_users(), bifocal dependency all imply multi-user trajectory

challenges.adversary_argument: Population layer is premature optimization for <10 users
challenges.verdict: SURVIVES — signal loss is irrecoverable; implementation cost is ~120 lines
challenges.key_evidence: LearnedWeights permanently lost on every Moka eviction; run_maintenance_all_users() already exists

challenges.adversary_argument: Auth is fine for current single-user MCP threat model
challenges.verdict: PARTIALLY SURVIVES — prerequisite for cross-user, not urgent for current deployment
challenges.key_evidence: RelevanceEngine.entity_index already leaks across users in multi-user; structural not theoretical

challenges.adversary_argument: MiniLM inversion at 384 dims is impractical; ~60% BLEU
challenges.verdict: SURVIVES — real attack is nearest-neighbor lookup (trivial), not inversion
challenges.key_evidence: name_embedding stored alongside EntityNode.name — oracle provides both cleartext and embedding

challenges.adversary_argument: Consolidation already strips PII in practice via keyword compression
challenges.verdict: SURVIVES with nuance — valid for low-salience PII, fails for high-salience (frequent names survive YAKE)
challenges.key_evidence: YAKE is statistical, not PII-aware; high-mention-count person names become top keywords

fixes.name: Collective Store

final_fix.description: Add collective/ directory at server root: global SlowStore (SQLite) for L3 non-Person graph topology + population_weights for mean LearnedWeights. Persist per-user LearnedWeights on Moka eviction. Seed new users from population mean. Add EdgeSource::CollectivePrior.

regressions_checked: Per-user retrieval (collective is read-only for queries)

fixes.name: Entity-Type-Aware PII Lifecycle

final_fix.description: Don't encrypt per-user graph (user owns their data). Instead: (a) accelerate Person-endpoint edge decay at 3× rate, (b) add EntityNode.pii_classification field set at NER time, (c) exclude pii_classification=Person from collective store writes, (d) scrub Person contributions from collective on forget_user() cascade.
final_fix.key_insight: Don't encrypt what you own. Prevent PII from reaching what you share.

regressions_checked: Per-user recall (high-salience colleagues survive via LTP)
GDPR compliance (forget_user cascade extended to collective)

fixes.name: Phased Auth Binding

final_fix.description: Add User type to types.rs. Add optional X-Veld-User-Id header validated against key→user binding. Fall back to request body user_id when absent (backward compatible). Deprecation warning on unbound access. MCP server updated to pass header.
final_fix.key_insight: Phased rollout — current clients unchanged, new clients get auth binding.

regressions_checked: MCP server (one env var becomes one header)

fixes.name: Graph Topology Over Embeddings

final_fix.description: Don't share raw embeddings cross-user at all. Share only structural graph topology (entity-pair existence + strength) through collective SlowStore. For knowledge gap detection, share Voronoi void centroids computed from non-Person entities only.
final_fix.key_insight: Highest-value cross-user features operate on graph structure and aggregate statistics, not raw embedding vectors.

regressions_checked: Cross-user clustering quality (graph topology sufficient for archetype clustering)
Knowledge gap discovery (void centroids of non-Person entities are safe)

fixes.name: Consolidation Privacy Gate

final_fix.description: Add NER pass on SemanticFact before collective store candidacy. If NER detects any Person entity in fact text, exclude from collective. Conservative: require ZERO Person entities for collective candidacy.
final_fix.implementation_cost: One NER inference per consolidated fact (~1ms)
final_fix.key_insight: Cost of false negative (name leaks) >> cost of false positive (safe fact excluded). Be conservative.

regressions_checked: Collective store completeness (some safe facts excluded by conservative filter — acceptable)

ranked_actions.action: Build collective store: persist LearnedWeights on eviction, add collective/ SlowStore, aggregate in maintenance loop
ranked_actions.rationale: 3-lens resonance (10 cross-products), survived adversarial, signal permanently lost on every cache eviction, implementation is ~120 lines in existing maintenance loop
ranked_actions.impact: HIGH (cold-start quality, population learning, knowledge transfer)

ranked_actions.action: Entity-type-aware PII lifecycle: pii_classification field on EntityNode, Person-endpoint exclusion from collective store, accelerated Person-edge decay
ranked_actions.rationale: 3-lens resonance, survived adversarial, prerequisite for ANY safe cross-user feature
ranked_actions.effort: MEDIUM (entity type filter + decay parameter + collective write gate)
ranked_actions.impact: CRITICAL (without this, cross-user features leak PII)

ranked_actions.action: Consolidation privacy gate: NER pass on SemanticFact before collective candidacy
ranked_actions.rationale: 2-lens resonance, survived adversarial with nuance, gates the consolidation→collective pipeline
ranked_actions.effort: LOW (one NER call per consolidated fact)
ranked_actions.impact: MEDIUM (prevents high-salience name leakage through keyword compression)

ranked_actions.action: Phased auth binding: User type, X-Veld-User-Id header, backward-compatible fallback
ranked_actions.rationale: 3-lens resonance, partially survived adversarial (not urgent for single-user but prerequisite for multi-user API)
ranked_actions.effort: HIGH (touches 31 handlers, MCP server, type system)

ranked_actions.action: Graph topology sharing (not embedding sharing): collective SlowStore holds only structural data, Voronoi void centroids for non-Person entities
ranked_actions.rationale: 3-lens resonance, survived adversarial, but resolved by R2's collective store (this is a constraint on R2, not a separate action)
ranked_actions.effort: LOW (constraint on collective store write filter — already part of R1/R2)
ranked_actions.impact: HIGH (eliminates embedding inversion attack surface for cross-user features)

unsurfaced_feature_clusters.name: Behavioral Archetype Engine
source_findings: A×B XP-07 (AB infra crosses user boundaries)
unsurfaced_feature_clusters.description: pattern_detection.rs + hook event streams + AB testing infrastructure = complete behavioral clustering pipeline. Tool usage distributions, emotional signatures, session rhythms, and topic drift patterns form user-agnostic feature vectors for archetype detection. No content needed.
prerequisites: R2 collective store (to persist archetype features)
R3 auth binding (to attribute patterns to users)
unsurfaced_feature_clusters.estimated_value: HIGH — enables personalized retrieval strategy per archetype without per-user training

unsurfaced_feature_clusters.name: Contested Gap Detector
unsurfaced_feature_clusters.description: Interference events around gap topology candidates reveal cognitively contested knowledge — topics where the user has conflicting beliefs. Cross-user, contested gaps shared by many users indicate genuinely hard problems vs. individual confusion.
prerequisites: R2 collective store (to store cross-user gap topology)
unsurfaced_feature_clusters.estimated_value: MEDIUM — novel quality signal for knowledge gap prioritization

unsurfaced_feature_clusters.name: Cross-User Knowledge Atlas
unsurfaced_feature_clusters.description: Population-level Voronoi analysis on the collective SlowStore reveals the topology of collective knowledge: where the population is dense (well-understood topics), where it's sparse (frontier knowledge), and where individual users have unique expertise (personal voids that are population-dense). This is CLIO-like insight at the knowledge level rather than the conversation level.
unsurfaced_feature_clusters.estimated_value: HIGH — answers 'what does the population know that I don't?' without any content sharing

unsurfaced_feature_clusters.name: Federated Decay Learning
unsurfaced_feature_clusters.description: DecayScales (FFT-learned access rhythms) are currently per-user. Population-averaged decay scales would reveal universal memory access patterns (e.g., Monday morning recall spikes, end-of-sprint topic switches). These are safe to aggregate because they contain no content — just temporal patterns.
prerequisites: R2 collective store (to store population decay scales)
unsurfaced_feature_clusters.estimated_value: MEDIUM — improves decay model for all users, especially cold-start

coverage_ratio.note: 76% of component × dimension space examined across 3 lenses and 35 cross-products

orthogonality_score.computation: Average |cos(θᵢⱼ)| across lens pairs weighted by coverage: A-B=0.21 (low overlap, high orthogonality), A-C=0.15 (lowest overlap), B-C=0.28 (modest overlap on cognitive science cluster)
orthogonality_score.interpretation: High orthogonality — lenses found genuinely different things at the same components

convergence_map.note: All fixes converged — no three-body oscillations in proposed solutions despite three-body systems in findings. Key: fixes avoid the three-body traps by operating on the selective membrane principle rather than trying to synchronize the three bodies.

meta_insights.deepest_structural_insight: The system needs a SELECTIVE MEMBRANE, not more isolation or less isolation. The current hermetic boundary is correct for security but incorrect for intelligence. The fix is not to remove the boundary but to add controlled permeability: L3 non-Person graph topology flows through; everything else stays contained. This is one architectural concept applied to one existing function (run_maintenance_all_users), not a redesign.
meta_insights.privacy_inversion_principle: Every Hebbian mechanism that improves individual recall (LTP, tier promotion, salience scoring) simultaneously increases the privacy cost of that memory's leakage. The most-remembered person is the hardest to anonymize. This is not a bug in the design — it is a fundamental tension between the Hebbian model and privacy. The resolution is entity-type-aware permeability: share the knowledge, not the knower.
meta_insights.relationship_to_clio: CLIO strips identity to find population patterns. Veld preserves identity for individual recall. The cross-user features proposed here create a THIRD mode: preserve identity locally, extract structure globally. The collective SlowStore is veld's equivalent of CLIO's cluster hierarchy — but built bottom-up from Hebbian graph topology rather than top-down from conversation classification. Where CLIO asks 'what are people doing?', cross-user veld asks 'what does the population know?'
